---
title: "PFE_test"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}

library(readr)

df1 <- read_csv("/Users/ben/Documents/GWU/ProgramForEcon/raw_gdp.csv")
df2 <- read_csv("/Users/ben/Documents/GWU/ProgramForEcon/raw_unemployment_sex.csv")
df3 <- read_csv("/Users/ben/Documents/GWU/ProgramForEcon/raw_unemployment_youth.csv")

```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed)

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

library(dplyr)

df1_avg <- df1 %>%
  group_by(TIME_PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(OBS_VALUE = mean(OBS_VALUE, na.rm = TRUE))

ggplot(df1_avg, aes(x = TIME_PERIOD, y = OBS_VALUE)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "World Average GDP Over Time", x = "Year", y = "GDP")

```

```{r}
ggplot(df1, aes(x = TIME_PERIOD, y = OBS_VALUE, group = geo, color = geo)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "GDP Over Time by Country", x = "Year", y = "GDP")
```

```{r}
ggplot(df1, aes(x = TIME_PERIOD, y = OBS_VALUE)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ geo) +
  labs(title = "GDP Over Time by Country", x = "Year", y = "GDP")
```

```{r}
ggplot(df1, aes(x = OBS_VALUE)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of GDP", x = "GDP", y = "Count")
```

```{r}
unique(df1$geo)
```

```{r}
west_big <- df1 %>%
  filter(geo %in% c(
    "United States",
    "Germany",
    "France",
    "United Kingdom",
    "Netherlands",
    "Belgium",
    "Austria",
    "Switzerland",
    "Denmark",
    "Norway",
    "Sweden",
    "Finland"
  ))
west_small <- df1 %>%
  filter(geo %in% c(
    "Germany",
    "France",
    "United Kingdom",
    "Netherlands",
    "Belgium"
  ))
eu_core <- df1 %>%
  filter(geo %in% c(
    "Germany",
    "France",
    "Italy",
    "Spain",
    "Netherlands",
    "Belgium"
  ))
```

```{r}
ggplot(eu_core,
       aes(x = TIME_PERIOD,
           y = geo,
           fill = OBS_VALUE)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C")

avg_growth <- eu_core %>%
  group_by(geo) %>%
  summarize(avg_growth = mean(OBS_VALUE))

ggplot(avg_growth,
       aes(x = reorder(geo, avg_growth),
           y = avg_growth)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip()

summary <- eu_core %>%
  group_by(geo) %>%
  summarize(
    avg_growth = mean(OBS_VALUE),
    volatility = sd(OBS_VALUE)
  )

ggplot(summary,
       aes(volatility,
           avg_growth,
           label = geo)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_text(nudge_y = 0.1)

ggplot(eu_core,
       aes(TIME_PERIOD,
           OBS_VALUE,
           group = geo)) +
  geom_line(color = "grey80") +
  geom_line(
    data = subset(eu_core, geo == "Germany"),
    linewidth = 1.5
  )

```

```{r}
ggplot(west_small,
       aes(x = OBS_VALUE,
           fill = geo)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4)

ggplot(west_small,
       aes(x = geo,
           y = OBS_VALUE)) +
  geom_boxplot()
```
